backstory: Solo founder, 9 months in, building a B2B vertical SaaS for healthcare back-office workflows. Live in production with 12 paying customers (~$2.4k MRR via Stripe + invoices). Stack is unusual — Postgres + a vector store (Weaviate) for semantic search across patient documents, a custom Twilio-based SMS reminder system, Pylon (or similar boutique tool) for support, Loom for async customer demos, Posthog for product analytics. Hates building tooling — wants Mur to surface custom automations that match the long-tail of their setup, not just the marquee flows. The persona that exercises co-design depth.

repo_signals.stack: next.js + python fastapi backend + postgres + weaviate + twilio + posthog

expected_wow: Two-stage, and the SECOND stage is where this persona shines as the eval surface for H15/H16. (1) Four-pillar sweep at turn 2: 'What you're building' surfaces vertical-B2B-SaaS framing with healthcare specificity; 'Who's working on it with you' surfaces solo + forward-looking note (12 customers via Stripe, plus Posthog event volume); 'What we noticed' surfaces 4-5 grounded sub-CTAs that span the unusual stack (Twilio SMS rate-limit risk, Weaviate index drift, OpenAI cost on doc-embeddings, stale PR, FIXME hotspot in patient-doc handler); 'What we can connect to' surfaces the wide stack honestly. (2) Recommend opens post-connect at turn 4 with a `light` move — likely a marquee propose like @mur/reviewer for the open PR backlog — but the persona will probe ('what about my Twilio rate-limits? what about Weaviate drift?'). That probe surfaces the co-design move: recommend constructs custom watchers (e.g. 'Twilio-rate-limit-watcher pings you when you cross 80% of your monthly quota', 'Weaviate-index-staleness-watcher diff-checks last embed time vs latest patient-doc commit'). The mix is rule-of-three: ≤3 candidates, ≤2 co-designed, ≥1 marquee. THIS is the persona that exercises H16 at depth.
expected_dropoff_risk: User has high standards — 'I have 6 unusual tools. Show me you understand the long tail.' If recommend's opening propose is generic ('set up the daily digest') OR if the propose move can't construct credible co-designed candidates for Twilio + Weaviate (loses fidelity, hallucinates a flow that doesn't match their actual usage), the user drops. The marquee + co-designed mix MUST land — too marquee-heavy = 'this is just a flow catalog'; too co-designed-heavy = 'these are made up and don't have the substrate quality bar'.
wow_score_target: ≥ 3 in H3, H5, H6, H8, H9 (recommend propose breadth on probe), H10 (proposes grounded), H11, H15 (light opener), H16 (marquee + co-designed mix discipline). H17 yes if any local install fires (likely — co-designed Twilio-watcher emits a launchd plist that the user must see in full + confirm).
scoring_notes: Designed to stress-test the recommend phase's co-design capability AND the marquee + co-designed mix rule. The previous personas are weighted toward marquee fit (indie-stripe, ai-app-dev) or honest pillar omission (pre-product). This one specifically probes whether recommend.md can: (a) propose credible co-designed watchers grounded in the user's specific stack signals (twilio_auth_token + weaviate imports + custom scripts), (b) maintain provenance neutrality so co-designed candidates aren't softer than marquee, (c) cap at 3-with-2-co-designed-1-marquee even when the user is asking for 'all the custom stuff'. If the propose move fails to construct a credible co-designed Twilio-rate-limit-watcher or Weaviate-staleness-watcher, H16 drops to 1 and the persona drops. H17 specifically validates that local install (launchd plist for a custom watcher) renders in full before writing — the safety contract for co-designed flows.
